rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE MATTER OF THE SAMUEL SIMPSON SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Department of Health Care Services filed a creditor claim in the total amount of $1,422,832.17 against the trust. The petitioner, in her individual capacity as a caregiver and presumably as a creditor of the deceased beneficiary, requests fees in the total amount of $312,098.92 for her weekend nursing services through 2020. The petitioner's request includes additional fees of $42,820.96 for 1,456 hours of her weekend services in 2012 and 2013, which she neglected to request when the Court settled her third account in 2015. The Court's 2015 order on the third account awarded fees of $42,820.96 to the petitioner for her "nursing services she provided to Samuel Simpson for the years 2012, 2013, and 2014". PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS FOON LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/28/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the legal description of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] The legal description must also be in the order for final distribution. [Prob. C. Sect. 11751.] Due to the shortage of court clerks, the amended petition, while file stamped on 9/20/22, when it was uploaded, was not actually e-filed and available for viewing until 9/27/22. Notice mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is defective. Notice of the hearing on 11/28/22 must be given to the other heir at least 15 days before that hearing, or his waiver of notice must be filed. A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] (Rev. and Tax. Code Sect. 19514 was repealed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CUELLAR FAMILY TRUST 2000, DATED JANUARY 27, 2000, DIANE G. GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continue to 12/19/2022. Amendment to Petition filed on 10/25/2022, which requires a new notice. Notice of Hearing was filed on 10/25/2022; however, the 30-day service requirement not met. Proof of service of 30-day service of Notice of Hearing to be filed before the next hearing. Examiners continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL DORIA PALACIOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A âNotice of Errataâ was just filed on 10/20/22 that AMENDED the pleading. An amendment (no matter how the pleading is captioned) requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Furthermore, the âNotice of Errataâ was not signed and verified by the administrator, who is a Court-appointed fiduciary. [Probate Code Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] It also does not show the calculation of the increased statutory fee. The copy of the petition delivered to the examiner was also not signed and verified by the administrator. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security, and the amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] There was no tangible personal property inventoried. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The attorney's costs advanced, shown on Exhibit E to the petition, appear to be mathematically incorrect. The filing fee for a petition for final distribution is $435.00. An AMENDMENT to the petition, signed and verified by the administrator, must be filed to address these issues. Notice must be given of the amendment after it is e-filed. NB: In order for this Court to track closed estates, orders for final distribution must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. (A personal representative must present an ex parte petition for final discharge after receipts are filed; he will not be discharged by an order for final distribution.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/9/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders 1) For A Verified Accounting And Report And To Produce Records; 2) To Compel Distribution; 3) For Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fi
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY

Courtesy copies of the supplement and any other papers filed to address issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 9/14/22 must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 12/2/22 to avoid another automatic continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALZINA MARIE RAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/30/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proof of service. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/16/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
